titleStmt.title: 288. Memorandum From the President’s Assistant for National Security Affairs (Brzezinski) to President Carter) to President Carter

div.head: 288. Memorandum From the President’s Assistant for National Security Affairs (Brzezinski) to President CarterSource: Carter Library, National Security Affairs, Brzezinski Material, Agency File, Box 6, Defense Department: 1/80. Top Secret; Sensitive. Sent for action.
div.p: Harold Brown has sent in a message requesting further guidance on a few items.

—Do we plan to follow up on Harold’s suggestion of trying to arrange a trip to Beijing by the Pakistani Defense Minister during Harold’s stay there? He assumes we will not and agrees with that but wants to know for sure.Carter checked the option “Do not follow up” and initialed “J.”

—Harold points out that the Chinese will be extremely interested in what support [1 line not declassified] we are prepared to provide the Afghan insurgents. [3½ lines not declassified]Carter checked the option “Approve; inform the Chinese” and initialed “J.”

—Harold suggests we brief selected Congressional Leaders on some of the key aspects of his visit and particularly the COCOM differential. He also suggests that we mention the OTH radar to Senator Glenn in “absolute confidence.” If you approve, we will coordinate an appropriate statement and approach to selected Congressional Leaders.Carter checked the Approve option and initialed “J.”
